ogTitle: Lajamanu vs Lake Bennett: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lajamanu and Lake Bennett.
heading: Lajamanu vs Lake Bennett — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lajamanu, Northern Territory and Lake Bennett, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lajamanu has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Lake Bennett (30/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lajamanu or Lake Bennett safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lajamanu has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Lake Bennett scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lajamanu scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Bennett scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lajamanu or Lake Bennett?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lajamanu has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
